title: Cyclodiscus tomentosus
taxonomy: Cyclodiscus tomentosus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclodiscus, which is a part of the order Gentianales. This classification places Cyclodiscus tomentosus within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The specific classification of Cyclodiscus tomentosus is still subject to some debate among botanists. However, its classification within the Rubiaceae family is widely accepted.

intro: Cyclodiscus tomentosus is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclodiscus tomentosus include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, such as iridoids and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever and skin conditions, as well as to support immune function.
